title: GUI deployment: Windows code signing (SignPath OSS or Azure Trusted Signing)
description: Apply for SignPath.io OSS sponsorship (free for qualifying OSS) OR set up Azure Trusted Signing (pay-per-signature, no HSM). Wire the chosen mechanism into gui_builder.yml as a post-bundle step that signs both the bundled .exe and the produced .msi/.exe NSIS installer. Update the README's Code signing section to document the path actually taken.
acceptance_criteria: SmartScreen no longer flags freshly downloaded installers; signed binaries verify with Get-AuthenticodeSignature.

title: GUI deployment: macOS Developer ID + notarization secrets
description: Acquire an Apple Developer ID Application certificate ($99/yr), generate a notarytool API key (.p8), and add the GitHub repo secrets the workflow already plumbs: APPLE_CERTIFICATE (base64 of the .p12), APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_TEAM_ID, plus either APPLE_ID + APPLE_PASSWORD (app-specific) or APPLE_API_ISSUER + APPLE_API_KEY + APPLE_API_KEY_PATH (API-key flow). gui_builder.yml's Configure macOS signing step activates automatically once APPLE_CERTIFICATE is set.
acceptance_criteria: Signed and notarized .dmg from a tagged release launches on a fresh macOS without the xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine workaround.

title: Fix R-1336mzz(Z) enthalpy/entropy reference state mismatch with REFPROP
description:
CI test failure in 'Enthalpy and entropy reference state' check (REFPROPMixtureBackend.cpp:2338-2339) for R-1336mzz(Z), introduced in PR #2781 (NIST IR 8570 mixture pairs).

Failing assertions:
- CHECK( std::abs(DH / h_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.901
- CHECK( std::abs(DS / s_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.766

Values:
- h_CP := 36244.058,  h_RP := 367081.535
- s_CP := 349.438,    s_RP := 1495.521
- delta_a1 := -22.6138265880399167
- delta_a2 := 14.6859102235818870
- RPName: R1336MZZZ, RRP=8.31445980, RCP=8.314462618

The CoolProp ideal-gas Helmholtz spec (IdealGasHelmholtzCP0PolyT with c=4.0 plus two PlanckEinstein terms at v=736, 2299) is producing h/s that disagree with REFPROP's R1336MZZZ at the reference state by an order of magnitude. Likely a reference-state offset (a1/a2) or cp0 coefficient discrepancy with the REFPROP FLD file.

Run: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/actions/runs/24936263809/job/73022241015

title: GUI deployment: bundle third-party license notices in an About dialog
description: Wire `cargo about` (Rust deps) + `license-checker` (npm deps) into the build to emit an aggregated NOTICE/LICENSES file bundled into the .app/.exe/.deb. Add a minimal About dialog exposing it, plus the CoolProp version (git describe) and the Tauri/React versions. Required by MIT/Apache-2.0 dep license terms.
acceptance_criteria: About dialog reachable from the menu bar, displays CoolProp + Tauri + React versions and a scrollable third-party notices view.

title: GUI deployment: release tagging policy + automated GH release publishing
description: Decide a tag scheme (e.g. `gui-v0.1.0` to disambiguate from CoolProp library tags). Configure tauri-action in gui_builder.yml to publish a draft GitHub Release on `gui-v*` tags with all platform bundles attached. Document the release dance (bump version in package.json + tauri.conf.json + Cargo.toml, tag, push, edit + publish the draft) in wrappers/GUI/README.md.
acceptance_criteria: Pushing a gui-v0.1.0 tag yields a draft release with .dmg + .msi + .deb + .AppImage + .rpm attached, ready to publish.

title: GUI deployment: Tauri auto-updater with signed manifests
description: Enable the @tauri-apps/plugin-updater, generate a Tauri updater signing keypair (separate from the OS signing certs), and on tag push publish a signed update manifest (latest.json) + per-platform bundles to GitHub Releases. Wire an update-check call on app start with a user-visible 'restart to update' prompt. Document the updater key custody (likely a repo secret + offline backup).
acceptance_criteria: Running an older bundle finds the new release, verifies its signature, downloads, and on next launch is the new version.

title: GUI testing: macOS launch + screenshot in CI
description: Extend gui_builder.yml's smoke-test step to macos-latest (currently only Linux + Windows have a launch+screenshot test). Use `screencapture -x` to grab the desktop after launching the bundled .app, verify the binary stays alive past 8s, upload macos-startup.png alongside the existing linux-startup.png and windows-startup.png artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: macOS CI job captures and uploads macos-startup.png; failure path catches launch crashes.

title: GUI testing: end-to-end smoke flow via tauri-driver
description: Wire tauri-driver + WebdriverIO so we can exercise the actual app end-to-end: launch the binary, open the Property Calculator, compute Water at PT_INPUTS=(101325,300) and assert the resulting Dmass is within tolerance of 996 kg/m³; build a saturation table for R134a; switch to Humid Air, calculate at T=293.15 R=0.5, verify isolines render. Run on ubuntu-22.04 under Xvfb in CI.
acceptance_criteria: tauri-driver e2e test passes in CI; on failure, the test produces a screenshot + log uploaded as artifact.

title: GUI testing: Rust backend unit tests (state_manager, FFI)
description: Add #[cfg(test)] modules to wrappers/GUI/src-tauri/src/state_manager.rs and coolprop_ffi.rs covering: handle-table create/update/get/free lifecycle, error propagation when CoolProp returns a non-empty errstring, compute_saturation_table edge cases (n_points<2, empty params list, sweep boundaries), and HAPropsSI input validation via the probe-output trick.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test in src-tauri passes; tests run as a CI step on all three OSes.

title: GUI testing: frontend unit tests (Vitest + RTL)
description: Add Vitest + React Testing Library, write unit tests for components with non-trivial logic: PropertyCalculator (input-pair switching, basis-flip resets, compute error path), SaturationTable (column switching by basis, copy-TSV correctness), SaturationView (multi-panel add/close/state freeing), HumidAirCalculator (isolines toggle + clip at sat curve), and the hooks (useDraggableSplit clamp, useAbstractState lifecycle and free-on-unmount). Wire `npm run test` into gui_builder.yml as a pre-build step on ubuntu-22.04.
acceptance_criteria: npm run test passes locally and in CI; coverage on the listed components/hooks; failure surfaces as a CI red.

title: EPIC: CoolProp Desktop GUI (Tauri 2)
description:
Native cross-platform desktop GUI for CoolProp under wrappers/GUI/, built with Tauri 2 (Rust + native WebView), React/TypeScript/Vite, and Plotly. CoolProp is statically linked. Branch: ib/GUI.

Tabs implemented: Property Calculator (HEOS-only, 12 input pairs, draggable splitter), multi-panel Saturation Tables, Humid Air calculator with optional isolines through the computed state point.

CI (.github/workflows/gui_builder.yml) builds bundles on macOS / Windows / Ubuntu 22.04, runs post-build smoke tests (.deb on ubuntu:26.04 forward-compat, .msi silent install + launch), and captures startup screenshots on Linux+Windows.

Open work falls into two areas tracked by the children of this epic:
  - Testing: unit, e2e, visual-regression, performance.
  - Deployment: code signing (macOS + Windows), auto-update, package-manager submissions, release process.

Currently shipping unsigned bundles. macOS quarantine workaround documented in wrappers/GUI/README.md.

title: Assessment: decision memo (which method ships)
description: Synthesize benchmark + robustness results into a recommendation: ship Gernert, ship Chebyshev, ship both behind a switch, or keep production as-is. Include rollout plan and any flags/options needed.
acceptance_criteria: Memo committed to dev/ as a markdown file; key non-obvious findings saved via bd remember.

title: Assessment: robustness test set
description: Curated set of (T,p,z) points known to be hard: near-critical loci, three-phase-adjacent compositions, very-low-T heavy hydrocarbons, very-high-p, metastable wells. Each method runs against this set and is scored on convergence rate and accuracy vs REFPROP/known answer.
acceptance_criteria: Test set lives in dev/density_rootfinding_hardpoints.csv; harness consumes it and emits a per-method scorecard.

title: Assessment: unified benchmark harness
description: Extend dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp into a method-agnostic harness that runs all three solvers (production, Gernert, Chebyshev) over the same (T,p,z) grid, emitting CSV with method, timing, iterations, residual, and result density. Reuse existing CSV format where possible.
acceptance_criteria: One CSV per fluid system, one binary that takes --method=production|gernert|chebyshev.

title: Chebyshev: test suite
description: Mirror the Gernert test suite structure for solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev. Particular attention to behavior at the rectangle boundaries (must error rather than extrapolate, per design constraint).

title: Chebyshev: implement mixture density rootfinder
description: Implement the Chebyshev-based mixture density rootfinder per spec. Reuse the pure-fluid Chebyshev infrastructure from the superanc work where possible (worktree-cheb has prior art). Entry point: solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev, parallel to solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; smoke test on AGA-8 grid; behind a flag.

title: Chebyshev: spec mixture extension
description: Pure-fluid Chebyshev superancillary uses τ-only (or τ,δ on a rectangle) expansions. For mixtures, composition z is a new degree of freedom and a hard rectangle in (τ,δ) is composition-dependent (reducing functions). Three candidate strategies to evaluate before coding: (1) build the Chebyshev expansion at runtime per composition (slow setup, fast solve); (2) interpolate a tensor of Chebyshev coefficients in z (memory-heavy, fast setup); (3) hybrid — build only when a (T,p) call cannot be served by current expansion. Pick one, justify, write spec.
design: Constraint from memory: hard-rectangle in (τ,δ), no extrapolation; δ-compactification was tried and rejected (Jacobian blow-up). Target n=8–12 per piece, single-digit μs.
acceptance_criteria: Chosen strategy + rationale + pseudocode in --notes.

title: Gernert: test suite
description: Tests for solver_rho_Tp_Gernert covering: pure fluid (degenerates correctly), simple binary, AGA-8 mixture, near-critical, and known-hard low-T case. Cross-check density against solver_rho_Tp where the latter converges, and against REFPROP where available.
acceptance_criteria: Test cases live in src/Tests/ following the mixture-pair test pattern (see memory).

title: Gernert: implement density rootfinder
description: Implement the Gernert density-rootfinding scheme as a new entry point alongside solver_rho_Tp. Do NOT replace the production path yet — keep both available behind a flag for benchmarking. Suggested location: src/Backends/Helmholtz/HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.cpp, named solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; passes a smoke test on the AGA-8 Amarillo grid; behind a build/runtime flag so production path is undisturbed.

title: Gernert: spec the density-rootfinding algorithm
description: Gernert (2014) PhD thesis, TU Bochum. The thesis is already cited in VLERoutines.cpp for stability analysis but NOT for the density-rootfinding inner loop. Read §3 of the thesis (and the 2014 J. Chem. Thermo. paper if relevant), extract the algorithm: bracketing strategy, Newton/safeguarded steps, termination criteria, special-case handling near the spinodal. Produce a pseudocode spec in the issue notes.
design: Output is a spec, not code. The spec drives the implementation issue.
acceptance_criteria: Pseudocode + cited equation numbers + list of edge cases in --notes.

title: Baseline: characterize solver_rho_Tp failure modes
description: Before adding new solvers, document where the current path breaks. Run dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp pattern across: (1) Amarillo natural gas grid (already wired), (2) CH4/H2S, (3) a near-critical CO2/N2 case, (4) low-T heavy mix. Record per (T,p,z): wallclock, iteration count, which fallback tier resolved it, and any failure. Output CSV + a short markdown summary.
acceptance_criteria: CSV of grid results checked into dev/; one-paragraph failure-mode summary saved via bd remember.

title: EPIC: Mixture density rootfinding — Gernert vs Chebyshev assessment
description: Production solver is HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp (SRK guess + Newton, with solver_rho_Tp_global fallback). It fails or is slow in: near-critical regions, low-T heavy-hydrocarbon mixtures, and metastable branches inside the phase envelope. We have two candidate replacements: (A) Gernert's density-rootfinding scheme (Gernert PhD thesis 2014, TU Bochum), and (B) Chebyshev rootfinding extended from the pure-fluid superancillary work. Goal: implement both, benchmark on a common grid, and decide which (if either) replaces the production path.
design: Hard requirement: any replacement must be at least as robust as solver_rho_Tp_global on the existing test suite. Stretch: single-digit μs per evaluation in the bulk of the (T,p,z) domain.

title: GUI deployment: Flathub manifest + appstream metadata
description: Author a Flathub YAML manifest (org.coolprop.gui.yml) and an AppStream metainfo XML, build against the GNOME or Freedesktop runtime, submit to flathub/flathub. Provides sandboxed Linux distribution decoupled from distro versions. Handles the webkit2gtk runtime via the Flatpak runtime instead of declaring deb/rpm deps.
acceptance_criteria: flatpak install flathub org.coolprop.gui succeeds on a fresh Fedora and a fresh Debian; updates via flatpak update.

title: GUI deployment: winget package manifest
description: Once signed Windows MSIs publish to GitHub Releases, author and submit a winget manifest to microsoft/winget-pkgs (CoolProp.CoolProp.yaml + InstallerType: msi). Goal: `winget install CoolProp` works out of the box; updates via `winget upgrade`.
acceptance_criteria: winget install CoolProp succeeds on a clean Windows 11; winget upgrade installs subsequent releases.

title: GUI deployment: Homebrew Cask formula
description: Once signed/notarized macOS bundles publish to GitHub Releases, submit a Cask formula to homebrew/homebrew-cask (or maintain a tap under CoolProp/homebrew-coolprop) so users can install via `brew install --cask coolprop`. Cask should pin the SHA256 of the .dmg per release.
acceptance_criteria: brew install --cask coolprop installs the latest release on a clean macOS; updates flow via brew upgrade.

title: GUI testing: cold-start and core-flow performance benchmarks
description: Measure (a) cold start time from process spawn to interactive window, (b) saturation-table compute time for a 100-pt R134a table, (c) iso-RH chart background generation time, (d) iso-line generation through a humid-air state point. Establish baseline thresholds and gate PRs that regress them by >20%.
acceptance_criteria: perf benchmark suite committed; CI runs it; thresholds in a config file; regression > threshold fails CI.

title: GUI testing: visual regression diffs in CI
description: Use the existing CI launch-screenshot infrastructure as a baseline: capture canonical screenshots for each tab (Calculator, Saturation, Humid Air), commit them under wrappers/GUI/tests/baselines/, and diff PR screenshots against the baseline using pixelmatch or odiff. Flag PRs that materially change rendering. Threshold tuned to ignore antialiasing jitter across runner OS upgrades.
acceptance_criteria: PR-side diff job runs, posts a summary comment when the diff exceeds threshold, and links to the diff image in the run artifacts.

title: Add get_last_pt_flash_tier instrumentation
description: dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp references a get_last_pt_flash_tier() helper that does not exist in the codebase. Discovered during baseline work (CoolProp-aor): the bench was written against an instrumented build that was never committed. Add a thread-local counter in PT_flash that records which tier (Wilson-SS=1, GDEM=2, HELD=3) resolved the flash, and expose via a free function. Backfill into the new bench dev/bench_density_baseline.cpp so we can correlate timing tail with fallback tier.
acceptance_criteria: get_last_pt_flash_tier() returns 1/2/3 after a successful PT flash, 0 on error. Used by both bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp and bench_density_baseline.cpp.

value: Mixture density rootfinder baseline (CoolProp-aor): HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp(T, p, rho_guess=-1) fails on 25-65% of (T,p) cells where the full PT flash succeeds. Tested on CH4/H2S 70/30 (65% fail), CO2/N2 80/20 (49% fail), n-Decane/Methane 30/70 (25% fail). Failures cluster near phase boundaries — Householder4 starting from the SRK guess hits inflection points and bails. The full PT flash succeeds because update_TP() funnels into solver_rho_Tp_global with bracketed Brent. So 'solver_rho_Tp by itself' has a huge fragility gap relative to 'orchestrated by the flash.' Implication: a Chebyshev or Gernert replacement must either (a) be robust as a standalone rootfinder or (b) provide a similar bracket/dispatch wrapper. CSV+PNG artifacts in dev/bench_density_*.

CoolProp PT flash p95 times (CoolProp-aor baseline, 60x60 grids): CH4/H2S 9 ms, CO2/N2 44 ms (worst, near-critical band), n-Decane/Methane 8 ms. Max single-cell flash time 414 ms on CO2/N2 in near-critical region. Solver_rho_Tp isolated p95: 33-194 μs, max ~1 ms. So full flash dominates by ~100x, but inside the flash solver_rho_Tp is called multiple times per phase + per Newton iter, so cumulative cost is closer.
